steps.title: Use the inequality
steps.content: A point is a solution to $y \lt -4x + 4$ if its $y$-coordinate is less than $-4x+4$ for that point's $x$-coordinate.

steps.title: Test the choices
steps.content: Choice A — $(0,19)$ gives $19 \lt -4(0)+4$, or $19\lt 4$, which is false. Choice B — $(-1,22)$ gives $22 \lt -4(-1)+4$, or $22\lt 8$, which is false. Choice C — $(-3,0)$ gives $0 \lt -4(-3)+4$, or $0\lt 16$, which is true. Choice D — $(7,-1)$ gives $-1 \lt -4(7)+4$, or $-1\lt -24$, which is false. Therefore, only $(-3,0)$ is a solution, so C.
